preview_text:
The model favors England at 87%, leaving Ghana with virtually no winning probability in its framework. The market consensus across 40 sportsbooks is notably more cautious, pricing England at 77% and allowing Ghana an 8% chance of victory. That 10-percentage-point gap reflects material disagreement about the underdog's viability.

The model's implied distribution is heavily skewed toward an England win, with draws accounting for only 13% of outcomes. The sportsbook consensus distributes probability more evenly across the result space, assigning meaningful weight to a draw at 16% and a Ghana upset at 8%. The market view suggests a closer, more competitive match than the model's probabilities imply.

The model and market diverge substantially on Ghana's chances—the model effectively rules them out while sportsbooks and Polymarket both assign them 7–8% win probability. This is the primary tension in the forecast. The dashboard makes no claim about which assessment is correct; the divergence is flagged here to show that consensus and quantitative modeling do not align on the tail risk in this fixture.
